title: Can Your Body Really Rebuild Itself While You Sleep? 🤯
script: Right now, your bones are dissolving and regrowing at the same time. That's bone remodeling — and it never stops. Specialized cells called osteoclasts eat tiny sections of your skeleton while osteoblasts pour fresh bone right behind them. Your entire skeleton replaces itself roughly every 10 years. By the time this video ends, you've already lost and rebuilt millions of bone cells. The strangest part: if remodeling stopped for just 48 hours, your bones would become dangerously brittle. So — do you feel stronger knowing your skeleton is basically brand new, or does that freak you out?
search_summary: Human bone remodeling continuously replaces the entire skeleton roughly every 10 years through osteoclast and osteoblast activity.
comment_question: Does knowing your skeleton is basically brand new make you feel stronger or weirder? 🦴
source_note: National Institutes of Health, bone remodeling overview, 2020
